sections.description: Detailed justification and budget for defense funding requests.

fields.label: Description of Request:�
fields.draft_value:
* $9,000,000,000 in programmatic funding for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) in FY 2027
* Supports comprehensive research and mission operations across Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, and Heliophysics
* Restores investment levels required to maintain U.S. leadership in space exploration following recent budget disruptions
* Funds the execution of peer-reviewed National Academies Decadal Survey priorities
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request for the SMD topline amount of $9 billion as specified in the reference values for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Was this project included in the FY26 defense appropriations bill? If so, what was the amount?
fields.rationale: This field is for defense appropriations; NASA is funded under the CJS bill.

fields.label: FY27 President's Budget Request for this budget line: (If the PBR has not been released, please email this dollar figure with the budget line number when available)
fields.draft_value: Currently unknown (President's Budget Request has not been released)
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not yet been released.

fields.label: For FY27, what specific amount is needed to support this work?
fields.rationale: This is the specific funding level requested for the Science Mission Directorate in FY2027 to ensure program stability and leadership.

fields.label: Justification for inclusion of this requested funding. Describe this work, who the customer will be, and what milestones can be completed with FY27 funds. Note whether any FY25 or FY26 funds were applied to this work period.
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science invested $3,691,175.82 in FL-2 between FY2023 and FY2025, including support for 24 grants in the district, with 12 of those grants supporting Florida State University, Florida A&M University, and Tall Timbers Research, Inc.
* FSU researchers utilize SMD funding to refine satellite data for public health decisions and develop Earth climate modeling systems (MERRA-3)
* Counters rapid Chinese expansion in lunar and Martian exploration by ensuring U.S. missions remain on schedule and scientifically competitive
* Prevents further workforce erosion following the FY2026 budget volatility that impacted thousands of aerospace contractors and civil servants
fields.rationale: The justification uses specific FL-2 spending data including FSU and Florida A&M grants, plus broader strategic talking points about international competition and workforce stability provided in the context.

fields.label: Date this request was discussed with a member of Congressman Dunn's staff:
fields.rationale: The specific date of the constituent's meeting with staff is not provided in the context.

fields.label: What staff member did you meet with?
fields.rationale: The staff member's name is not provided in the context.

fields.label: How many fiscal years will this project require government funding to be completed?
fields.draft_value: Ongoing
fields.rationale: NASA Science is an ongoing federal agency mission requiring sustained annual appropriations.

fields.label: Are other Members supporting this language? If so, which Member(s)?
fields.draft_value: * Supported by a bipartisan coalition including Representatives Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: The reference values identify Bacon and Chu as the leads for the existing House support letter.

fields.label: Forms should be completed and submitted by close of business on Friday, March 6, 2026.
options: I understand and am ready to submit my request.
fields.draft_value: I understand and am ready to submit my request.
fields.rationale: Acknowledgment of the submission deadline.

fields.label: Justification for inclusion of this requested funding. Describe this work, who the customer will be, and what milestones can be completed with FY27 funds. Note whether any FY25 or FY26 funds were applied to this work period.
fields.draft_value: Funding of $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) is essential to restore momentum after FY 2026 funding disruptions. This investment supports over $3.6 million in active research in Florida's 2nd District, including major climate modeling and public health data refinement projects at Florida State University. FY 2027 funds will enable the US to maintain leadership against rapid Chinese expansion in space science, support thousands of high-tech jobs, and advance Decadal Survey priorities that provide a nonpartisan roadmap for discovery.
fields.rationale: Justification combines national strategic themes (US vs China competition) with specific local impact at FSU ($3.6M total across district) and workforce stability arguments.

issues.field_label: Justification for inclusion of this requested funding. Describe this work, who the customer will be, and what milestones can be completed with FY27 funds. Note whether any FY25 or FY26 funds were applied to this work period.
issues.issue: The draft claims 12 grants supported Florida State University, Florida A&M University, and Tall Timbers Research, Inc., but the fact sheet confirms 10 (FSU) + 1 (FAMU) + 1 (Tall Timbers) = 12 grants total. This part is correct. However, the claim that 12 of the 24 grants went to these three specific entities is verified by the fact sheet, but the fact sheet explicitly lists the total number of grants for these three entities as 12 (10+1+1). The draft is consistent with the fact sheet.

issues.field_label: Justification for inclusion of this requested funding. Describe this work, who the customer will be, and what milestones can be completed with FY27 funds. Note whether any FY25 or FY26 funds were applied to this work period.
issues.issue: The draft states FY2026 budget volatility 'impacted thousands of aerospace contractors', which is a qualitative claim not explicitly enumerated in the provided fact sheet data.

validation.summary: Passed with warnings: 1 low, 1 medium issue(s).

top_grants.description: The overarching goal of this project is to improve public health decision making on ci investment through refining satellite observations and portable statistic modeling and close stakeholder engagement.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Refining satellite data for improved public health decisions.

top_grants.description: The next major reanalysis from the gmao  i.E.  Merra-3  will include ocean and sea-ice components coupled to the atmosphere and is a significant leap forward from merra-2.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing the MERRA-3 integrated Earth climate modeling system.

top_grants.description: 21-ccst21-0025  partitioning solid and liquid precipitation over global mountains using cloudsat and trmm/gpm observations
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Analyzing global mountain precipitation types using satellite data.

top_grants.description: The proposed work would investigate the underexplored record of early animals from the canadian cordillera by completing three main objectives. The first two objectives included searches for  and documentation of  paleontological  sedimentological and stratigraphic information preserved within three shelf-to-slope transects at multiple field sites. These local studies would then be integrated into a regional stratigraphic and geochronological framework from which the proponents could evaluate potential factors controlling the distribution of ediacaran fossils by testing the hypothesis that varying faunal composition is entirely the result of evolutionary change with time. A third objective would aim to place these proposed findings within a global context  intended to provide new constraints on the timing and environmental setting of the evolution  diversification  and subsequent demise of the first animal communities on Earth.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Analyzing ancient animal fossils defining early Earth diversification.
